reviews.review_text:
Bought it and played it together with a friend from start to end through both base game and Expansion.
Absolute Blast of a game, Gameplay wise as well as with its Music and Roster of Monsters this has become one of my Favorite Monster Hunter Games currently out there!
I can Highly recommend it! 
Especially if you have a Friend or Multiple Friends to come along that journey!!

Portable 3rd was my last game of Monster Hunter. 

And Sunbreak includes a small homage to P3rd. 

Therefore RiseBreak is the last game I'll ever need.

It has been a difficult game for me like but at the end, as of now, at 52 hours, after going back and forth a few times, it seems I have come in terms with the parts I don't like.
First, the basic monster hunter format, I have no complaints what so ever. Love the simplicity and directness of the flow of gameplay loop
Second, the combat, love the changes, especially how I can actively close gaps qucikly and continue the assault against any monsters. It is definitely different from world, but I don't mind this new flavour. As I said, I actually like it a lot
Art style, sound effects are sweet. Personally, I really love how gamey it feels on a steam deck

Now, the problems I had were the spirit birds and the rampage. No point in going into too much details as by itself, they aren't bad mechanics or system, but personally I just don't like it. Especially rampage. That was my reason I didn't play this for so long. But, I have found a place in me to accept these and I am still having fun with the rest of the things the game has to offer

PS - I did hear about somthing called curious crafting, I haven't reached that point yet, but lets see. Based off of what I have heard, that might just be the barrier that I won't be able to cross. But until then, I will continue to enjoy this game

I like how it didn't waste too much time giving a tutorial, so you can jump straight into fighting monsters. I don't usually play many ARPGs, but I've enjoyed Monster Hunter Rise and will continue to do so. Every fight is good, and I like learning the monsters' mechanics. Performance of the game is also nice, and the loading times are fast.

I swing around my hammer and a monster dies, I take it's parts and make a bigger hammer, just to kill a bigger monster. I take it's parts and make an even bigger hammer, which allows me to kill even bigger monsters.

Yeah that's basically the whole game, very enjoyable. :)

Weirdly enough, after playing MH Wilds, this game became better. Maybe I missed the mobility, but I certainly missed the rapid fire just being an inherent trait of the LBG than a dumb gauge in Wilds. Wilds felt like they were trying to recreate World's success while incorporating Rise's heightened mobility but failed to do both effectively.

Sorry I ever cussed out the dumb qurio farming. This game rocks. It only stinks when you get one-shot but that ends up being a skill issue so that's w/e.

I had been a player for this game since it released on PSP. I enjoyed all the game mechanics and allow myself to communicate rotations and execute plays with friend teammates. It's my birthday today and I will retire for this game for now and will use the skills and learnings to this game to real life and will climb out of poverty. I will return soon.

Top Tier MH entry. 

To me it's like a straight to the point, focused approach that takes a bit from the old generations and a bit from the new gen and it seems to work perfectly for it. Specially the minimalistic story, let's be honest, I don't quite much care about the feelings of Arkveld or why this or that kid is obssesed with it, I want to hunt that MF, simple as that. The elder says hey a rampage is coming and... say no more buffed old timer I gotcha, then you head straight into the fight.

I also appreciate that each stage is considerable smaller than say MH World or Wilds, to a certain extent I felt that in those two games the maps were bloated with dead areas while Rise makes good use of each small region.

The game also takes advantage of the RE engine, and since the maps are small it doesn't suffer from low fps unlike Dragons Dogma or WIlds. Textures are a huge step up from the Switch version and fps can be uncapped for those with higher refresh rate monitors.

Rise and Sunbreak are a no brainer, extra recommended while on sale.

i like how it is great as a first game yes i have only played this in franchise but compared to what i seen in worlds and wilds the map is smaller the damage number is higher which makes monster hunts easier. my only problem is that the village quests are the only way to see the cutscenes for the monsters that are low rank
Great game, tho I ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ despise Primordial Malzeno. I just want to be able to hit him :( but he doesn't stop spamming combos and running away.

ngl the game is fun and hard at the same time but thats actually the good part about this game,just killing some monsters,get some items,quest,earn coins from it [sadly i forgot what it called again],so yeah overall this game is good
great game, not that open world but still has very fun fighting mechanics
in the hunt for monster, we must take care to become monsters ourselves.

One of my favorite entries in the series is Monster Hunter Generations Ultimate, and this absolutely fills the spot GU left behind. Though nothing can replace my beloved Valor Style Charge Blade. Shi ain't hit the same.

I've been playing Monster Hunter for a very long time, through so many generations, and Rise: Sunbreak is easily one of my favorites. It honestly makes me sad when people call this a "spin-off" just because it was made by the Portable Team. That couldn't be further from the truth. 
Playing this game instantly brings back beautiful nostalgia, especially because the fast-paced action and flashy styles heavily remind me of my time back in MHGen / MHXX. It brings me right back to the good old days when my friends and I would sit together, sharing our handheld screens, shouting in panic, and cheering after defeating tough monsters in those older generations. 
Even though some of those old hunting buddies have moved on with life, those memories remain alive every time I load into this game. This title is a massive mainline entry, and it's actually one of Capcom's best-selling games of all time for a reason. It captures the true soul of the franchise: gathering with your friends, sharing laughs, and making unforgettable memories together. The combat in Sunbreak is incredibly fast and satisfying, and the endgame build customization is so deep. If you're a new player or an old veteran looking for an amazing hunting experience with friends, don't listen to the internet gatekeepers. Just grab your weapon, visit Kamura and Elgado, and enjoy the ride. 10/10!
wasnt sure if I liked it. After playing MH Wilds I can definitively say I do.

Controls took me a little while to get used to, but this is such a fun game! Character customization is fun, the palamute and palicoe companions are very cute and helpful + customizable, and the monster designs are EPIC! I love games with resource gathering and learning about the different ways to kill or trap monsters for materials. If you enjoy games like Skyrim and Witcher 3 then you'll probably like this one!

This game is great for me because this is actually my first mh game. Graphic is decent it's not that bad and the combat have the most fun thing in this gaming ngl, being able to just fly around with gunlance weapon with wirebug, ts just great overall. The monster in this game actually so badass and menacing. 
Also the pc requirements for this game are pretty low for modern mh game so it's basically run smoothly with my lenovo yoga laptop. 
Overall this game is great place for beginner to start with as this game really not demanding high pc requirement and have one of most fun gameplay I've ever played with my friends

I played this multiplayer with my friends as that should you do for beginner at mh because played single-player genuinely just horrendous experience for beginner. If you don't have any firends to play with just join monster hunter community. This game has one of friendliest community in gaming,

That's about it for my humble opinion based review, sorry about bad english as that not my first language.

Played about 300 hours on Xbox, and 200 on switch.

Favourite monster of all time. Love em or hate em, the wirebugs are a very unique mechanic for MH. Having different moves that you have to manage a cooldown for as well as burst movement for all weapons is really neat, and keeps the game fast paced.

The early game is quite easy in my opinion, but starts to become a challenege once you unlock some of the later high rank monsters. The game really opens up once you reach the expansion and unlock master rank. The different build possibilities made available through Qurious Crafting is unmatched in any game I have played.

The fights are fun, all the monsters are iconic, and I just really really REALLY love Rise

Joguei o suficiente pra fazer o primeiro final ([spoiler]depois do final falso, mas[/spoiler]
com o qual o jogo foi lançado originalmente) e chegar na expansão (que vale a pena). É bem bom. Meu terceiro Monster Hunter (joguei um pouco do original no PS2 e de PSP). O foco é em caçar os monstros que são basicamente chefes, e "grindar" material pra fazer equipamentos melhores pra caçar monstros mais fortes: é basicamente o ciclo. Ouvi dizer que é o mais fácil, rápido e que mais tem ação (tipo anime, principalmente levando em consideração o jogo foi feito com a cultura Japonesa como inspiração e tema) do que os outros dá série. Aparentemente qualquer um que esteja acostumado com jogo de ação consegue fazer 95% do conteúdo sozinho (tem "pets" que te ajudam se você quiser e parece que também NPC humano no DLC ), 5% seria só pra veterano ou gente muito esforçada. O online não me interessava mas no fim acabei jogando e é bem bom sim (afinal, é talvez o principal foco da venda da série), além do mais é só cooperativo, então não tem como ser desvantagem - na verdade facilita bastante e em 2026 ainda tem gente jogando. Bem customizável, com muita variedade, META pra dar e vender com alguns drops enlouquecedores pra quem é viciado em min-maxing. Originalmente de Nintendo Switch, então os gráficos não são ruins e não requer um PC muito forte pra rodar com "settings" alto. Não raro tem sale boa, então vale a pena esperar pra comprar logo com o Sunbreak.

Monster Hunter Rise was my first Monster Hunter game and I will always love it I can not fairly review the game because I have such a soft spot for the base game. I have yet to get to Sunbreak but as far as base game goes if you want a mobile MH experience this is it. I have yet to play Sunbreak but will update my review because I hear it is peak

As expected of Monster Hunter franchise, great hunt!
Also Amatsu... would

Makes me want to play Monster Hunter World. The controls are hard to get used to at first and the adjustment from previous titles, because Rise was made for the switch. RIse has some great monsters and some very bad ones. Also the siege mode in hard mode is awful, I don't know who thought that was a good idea in Monster Hunter. it's worth a try if you can get it on sale.

Prob the best Monster Hunter in terms of gameplay loop.

The early game is just fodder but things really start picking up after MR.
Better than World, Wilds is better but Sunbreak is goated
a great game, still evolving, fun and very very time consuming (in a good way) best way to spend excess time

I had the misfortune of playing this after Worlds, which made it seem like a downgrade in almost every sense. Now that I play Sunbreak, which expands on the Silkbind techniques and monster riding, adding new monsters to the mix (which aren't the best I've seen but still enjoyable), I've found its appeal. Servers are pretty much dead, but with NPC hunters helping you, it should be more than enough to beat the game.
Enjoyable both solo and multiplayer. Run well in Steam Deck.

The best game in a great series.  Combat feels great, not dumbed down like Wilds but with plenty of room for optimization, the wirebugs and silkbind skills are great additions, there's plenty of content, and while it may be less pretty than Wilds, it still looks, sounds, and runs good.  I will say that the Rampage quests are a weak point in the game, but even they aren't too bad.
One of the best monster hunter series I've ever played. Already have like 3k hours on switch and I'm still enjoying playing this.
Sorry but this is the first MH game that i actually "finished", playing alone is hard but its super fun together, i'm suprised that even i was hooked to this
Really worth the time. The combat feels fast and satisfying, the hunts are exciting, and every session left me wanting one more quest. Definitely fun from start to finish.
Fun game, no tenderizing needed. In the endgame with no end in sight. Zero frame drops, which is good considering how fast paced this game can become.
Hunter is a pretty cool guy, eh kills montsers and doesnt afraid of anything
Goated game, its fun replayable and good overall with the story telling and gameplay with it's different mechanics
A fantastic monster hunter although collecting spirit birds gets pretty tiring after a good while

Its a good game but the randale quest are unbalanced and just bad, after some point without this unbalance i would vote up but now i do this! even dark souls is more fair at randale quest after some point. but everthing else perfektion like allways monster hunter is great.

Good: 
1. Sunbreak is a paradise for Dual Blades, Longsword, and perhaps Switch Axe mains.
2. High build variety with a wide selection of skills.
3. Cats and dogs have highly customizable skill sets.
4. NPC Teammates in Sunbreak.
5. A lot of Mods for PC.


Bad: 
1. Monsters attack with insane speed and spam too many AOE attacks. They are constantly jumping around like crazy, which becomes deeply annoying. The learning curve is frustrating and punishing. If you want to learn monster patterns or practice counters, it feels terrible.

2. Hardly any actual players online. The main community is on Switch, and there’s no cross-platform compatibility. To make it worse, Steam version is region-locked.

3. Disappointing design for Greatsword in Sunbreak. The playstyle heavily relies on waiting for counters to deal any meaningful damage. It feels too passive, especially when monsters move at such an insane speed. As a GS main, I prefer Wilds' Offset Attack design, even though GS currently struggles in endgame high-difficulty missions due to bugs, slower attack speed, and lower overall DPS.

 
Conclusion: Some love this game, others hate it. I think it's above average, but not a masterpiece. it's definitely worth giving it a try when it's on sale.
se brincar até o primeiro monster hunter, aquele de PS2, tem uma jogabiliade melhor que esse lixo
good old monster hunter loop of kill and make newer weapons and armour
Fustration, No worries beat up innocent looking monsters, problem solved :D

Peak combat, really satisfying once you get the hang of it. The monster roster is absolutely great. A lot of good returning monster (Especially Amatsu, been a long time since Portable Third) and quite a lot of good new monster. But still, I kinda hope they include Agnaktor and Ukanlos in the roster.

The innovations they brought with this title are great too. Well, maybe except spiribirds. While it can be fun exploring the map to collect them, a lot of times it just straight up a chore. You can ignore them tho, if you're confident enough with your skill. 

Overall, very recommended, especially if you don't have much time to play.
I have had dango-making cat for one day, but if anything happened to them, I would hunt everyone in the village and then myself.

♥♥♥♥ I HATE THIS GAME. Like wtf do you mean the monster flees 12 ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ TIMES in one hunt??? It's not fun at all! This "always flee every 1 minute" mechanic won't give me any feel of achievement like "Owh he ran away, hell yeah it means that it's scared and hurt!" or any dopamine at all. It only ANNOYS me to the point that I regret installing this game, lowkey even regret buying this even at disc price. 

If you wanna play Monster Hunter, just play the old gens. Or World if you want the more modern one or if you are new to the series.
ถ้ามึงเล่นWorldมาก่อน ภาคนี้จะแย่มาก นอกจากมอนเยอะ กับการควบคุมสุดจะบรรลัยกัลก็ไม่มีอย่างอื่นเลย แต่มึงเล่นภาคนี้แรกเป็นแรก แล้วค่อยเล่นWorld ภาคนี้จะดีขึ้นมาทันที
Another underwhelming copy and paste mechanics from the Monster Hunter team. If only there was any creativity left at the studio.

I came from Wilds so I dunno how these older games are supposed to go, but the mobility here is so dull. Also, I find that in this game there is more reading than actual gameplay. When I did get to the gameplay, I spent a while to hunt a monster and it didn't even give me enough time to carve the rewards. so after all that, this game is being shelved for a bit
awesome monster designs, great soundtrack, beautiful scenery, and a diverse weapon system!!

Honestly, after the expansion came out this became my favorite modern monster hunter.  It doesn't have the same gimmick system that takes away your ability to play how you want.  The monster variants and endgame scaling are genuinely the best in the modern games.  If we are being honest, the master rank monsters and even anomaly monsters feel good to fight.  You don't get stuck in a 30 minute fight because you didn't take the weapon and build capcom wanted you to use.  This is the only modern monster hunter that has that classic, play the way you want to, feel and doesn't punish you for doing so.  I do admit when I wrote this I forgot about rampage quests and didn't do any more than necessary.
Great so far. underrated in the Monster Hunter series

This game is really good and a lot of fun, but it shouldn't be your first Monster Hunter game. They take too long to let you fight the fun monsters and unlock all the alternate attacks for your weapons. I think I would have bounced off if I wasn't sure it would get more interesting later. It's not, like, awful early on or anything but it starts slow. Later on and into Sunbreak it's great fun, more than worth the slow start.

as much as i disliked base rise, sunbreak just hits it out of the park, i have over 1k hours in ps and i aim to do the same on steam, not the best monster hunter game, but the one i have the most fun in for sure

fantastic game (coming from a world lover)
dont believe what people say about the graphics they're fine. this game has the fastest and deepest combat of the series. and features an arsenal of QOL feature that i wish were in the last game (faster gathering. faster traversal with the wirebugs and the dog. the return of the "key quest" system which leads to open progression and etc).
the new silckbugs are so fun to play with and you even get you own DOG :)

if youve played the older games (especially p3rd) then this is the game for you.
features returning monsters that were absent in world and brings back the old but welcoming aesthetic of the older games (familiar maps. weapon designs. monsters). youll feel right at home.

negatives: the graphics and sound design are rough and personally i didnt find it as immersive as world nor do i like the japanese style of the game (biased) + the maps are very open and feels kinda empty when you're playing solo which makes sense cuz theyre meant to be arenas for you to hunt in em.

overall: this game is really fun and you should give it a try even if you dont like the japanese aesthetic

this game is phenomenal, combat feels more fluid than before and I like how you can travel with a dog now to make hunts faster, especially when your lookin for a quick hunt. The only thing I dislike about this game is the need to collect spiritbirds to maximize your stats which feels like a chore that you have to do before fighting a monster

So far feels less impactful than Monster hunter worlds, however i still havent gotten to the dlc which many people say carries the game. Still a great game nonetheless, will have high hopes for the next monster hunter game too.
